title: A REAL PAIN
logline: Two mismatched Jewish-American cousins travel to Poland to honor their recently deceased grandmother, joining a Holocaust tour that forces them to confront their family history and their complicated relationship with each other.
protagonist: David and Benji Kaplan (dual protagonists - cousins)
antagonistOrConflict: The cousins' unresolved grief, guilt, and family trauma; Benji's self-destructive behavior and David's repressed emotions
tone: Melancholic, darkly comedic, introspective with moments of levity

actBreaks.description: Setup ends when cousins meet tour group at hotel restaurant and begin their journey

validation.ruling: Scene #22 is designated as an act break but also serves as the inciting incident launching the tour. Scene #24 at the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument marks the true end of Act 1 setup and beginning of the journey proper.

actBreaks.description: Second act ends after Majdanek concentration camp visit - the emotional climax of their historical journey

validation.ruling: Scene #84 is designated as an act break but the more significant turning point is Scene #105 (p84, 83%) where the cousins have their rooftop conversation overlooking Majdanek, representing the transition into the final act of resolution and departure.

actBreaks.description: Final act begins when they reach grandmother's actual hometown and house

validation.ruling: Scene #116 at their grandmother's childhood home (p93, 92%) properly marks the end of Act 3, representing the completion of their heritage journey and emotional resolution before returning home.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Character-driven journey structure with geographic and emotional progression from New York to Warsaw to Lublin to grandmother's village, mirroring internal journey from surface conflict to deeper family trauma
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Deliberately paced with extended dialogue scenes and quiet character moments. Heavy concentration of short transitional scenes in the middle section during travel sequences. Extended rooftop conversation scene serves as emotional centerpiece.

incitingIncident.description: At the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument, James begins the tour by addressing myths about Jewish resistance during WWII.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. WARSAW GHETTO UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #22 is an act break and cannot be selected. Scene #24 at the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument (p25, 25%) is the true inciting incident where James begins the Holocaust tour by addressing myths about Jewish resistance. This launches the central dramatic journey of the screenplay and occurs at the perfect structural position at the end of Act 1.

midpoint.description: On the hotel roof overlooking Warsaw, they share weed and Benji reflects on their past adventures in New York, remembering how David would always fall asleep during their all-night walks.

validation.ruling: Scene #84 is an act break and cannot be selected. Scene #43 on the Warsaw Hotel roof (p41, 41%) serves as the midpoint where David has a contemplative moment alone on the rooftop at night, representing the emotional center and turning point of his internal journey during this heritage trip.

climax.description: The cousins emerge onto the quiet rooftop overlooking Lublin after struggling with a rusty industrial door.

validation.ruling: Scene #104 on the Lublin rooftop (p84, 83%) is the emotional climax where the cousins share their final meaningful moment together, smoking and reflecting while overlooking the city that contains the Majdanek concentration camp. This creates the story's emotional peak before their departure.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Opening credits over Benji sitting alone at JFK Airport, looking melancholy as travelers pass by, establishing the film's tone.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David hurriedly leaves his Brooklyn apartment, calling Benji about airport timing and early arrival requirements.

scenes.heading: EXT. BQE - DAY
scenes.summary: David's Uber stuck in traffic on the BQE highway, building travel anxiety.

scenes.heading: INT. UBER - DAY
scenes.summary: David leaves multiple frantic voicemails for Benji from the Uber, showing his anxious nature and need for control.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: David arrives at JFK still leaving messages when Benji surprises him from behind; they reunite warmly but David tries to rush the check-in process.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPO RT HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk through the airport hallway toward security, David discretely throwing away yogurt.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT SECURITY — DAY
scenes.summary: At airport security, David nervously watches Benji's bag while Benji charmingly flirts with TSA agent Jasmine, showcasing their contrasting personalities.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT GATE - DAY
scenes.summary: At the gate, Benji takes David's trail mix and claims he's been 'great' lately, with unspoken tension about recent months they avoid discussing.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: On the plane, Benji gets the window seat and asks David to take the uncomfortable middle seat.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: David cramped in middle seat discusses his digital ad sales job while Benji dismisses it as something 'everybody hates,' revealing their different life paths.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During the night flight, David stays awake missing his son Abe while watching videos, as Benji sleeps peacefully with empty wine bottles.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Exhausted at Warsaw airport, Benji has a dark mood and declines breakfast, showing his emotional volatility.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT CUSTOMS AREA - MORNING
scenes.summary: David nervously watches Benji's bag go through customs while Benji remains completely unfazed by security.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - MORNING
scenes.summary: After clearing customs, Benji's mood suddenly shifts from bleak to bright when he spots their Heritage Tours driver.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MORNING
scenes.summary: In the car to Warsaw, David watches videos of his son while Benji stares out the window, both dealing with separation and homesickness differently.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW - MORNING
scenes.summary: They arrive at the upscale Warsaw Central Hotel for their heritage tour.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Checking in at the hotel, Benji receives a mysterious package that's been waiting for days, puzzling David.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: In the hotel hallway, Benji affectionately tells David how happy he is to be there together.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In their hotel room, Benji compliments David's feet and reminisces about their grandmother's feet, connecting family memories to their trip.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: The cousins walk down the hotel hallway, Benji refreshed while David remains exhausted from travel.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW LOBBY - MORNIN G
scenes.summary: In the hotel lobby, they meet James, their young British tour guide, who intercepts them before the group meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: James introduces David and Benji to their small heritage tour group and explains his background as an Oxford scholar of Eastern European Jewish history, while Benji immediately charms everyone and David observes his cousin's social ease.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction to the tour group and establishment of group dynamics that will drive much of the film's interpersonal conflict

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Montage of Warsaw's bustling city life set to Chopin's racing Etude, establishing the setting.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW GHETTO UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument, James begins the tour by addressing myths about Jewish resistance during WWII.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks along a street lined with Soviet-era apartment blocks.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji buys water for the group but has no money, requiring David to pay, showing their financial dynamic.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASINSKI GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: In Krasinski Garden, Benji philosophizes about alternate histories where they might have been Polish Jews, while David reflects on seeing Hasidic men in New York.

scenes.heading: EXT. WALICOW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through Warsaw's ruins mixed with modern reconstruction, seeing the contrast of old and new.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAC GRZYBOWSKI - D AY
scenes.summary: Benji playfully skips across stones in a pond while the patient group waits for his antics to finish.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAC GRZYBOWSKI CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: At a cafe, Benji reveals he's learned intimate details about Marcia's recent divorce and dating life, showing his ability to connect with people quickly.

scenes.heading: EXT. KREDYTOWA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through a bohemian neighborhood street in Warsaw.

scenes.heading: EXT. Ś WI Ę TOJERSKA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group passes a fragment of the old Warsaw Ghetto Wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Warsaw Uprising Monument, James explains Polish-Russian-German conflicts while Benji insists on taking inappropriate photos with the bronze soldiers, embarrassing David and revealing his lack of social boundaries.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's inappropriate behavior at a solemn memorial reveals his inability to read social situations and begins to strain his relationship with David

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group returns to the hotel with good energy from the day, though David feels somewhat left out.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR — NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the hotel elevator, Benji warmly bids goodnight to the group members who respond fondly to him.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL HOTEL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins walk down the hotel hallway toward their room after the elevator ride.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji asks David to come into the cramped bathroom for a private conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the bathroom, Benji intensely thanks David for the trip and acknowledges David's social discomfort, while David denies being uncomfortable with groups.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji wants to smoke weed but the window only opens slightly; he pressures exhausted David to stay awake and smoke together on their first night.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David and Benji ride the elevator with Benji carrying a blunt, both in good spirits about their smoking plan.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY OF TOP FLOOR — NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the top floor, they find a door with an alarm warning, but Benji convinces reluctant David to try the stairwell instead.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL STAIRWELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins giggle as they race up the hotel stairwell to access the roof.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOF — NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the hotel roof overlooking Warsaw, they share weed and Benji reflects on their past adventures in New York, remembering how David would always fall asleep during their all-night walks.
scenes.turningPointNote: Intimate moment revealing their shared history and the patterns in their relationship, with hints of deeper issues

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins ride the elevator back down in contemplative silence after their rooftop conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In their hotel room, stoned and eating minibar snacks, they apologize to each other - Benji for calling David a lightweight, David for always falling asleep during their adventures.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes up to find Benji still sleeping surrounded by empty vodka bottles, showing Benji's continued drinking.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David whispers on FaceTime with his wife Priya about their son, then takes medication while getting ready, showing his routine and homesickness.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David tries to wake sleeping Benji, who has a pained expression even in sleep, as they need to catch their train.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Benji reluctantly follows David through the hotel hallway, moving slowly and reluctantly.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: The group waits outside the hotel as David apologizes for being late, while Benji's charismatic energy immediately returns when he sees the others.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through Warsaw train station, with Benji taking everything in with childlike wonder

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW TRAIN STATION PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: James leads the group to first class train seats, explaining it's included in the tour fee. David looks happy while Benji appears skeptical about the accommodation

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: External shot of the train moving southeast through Central Poland's countryside

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji becomes uncomfortable riding first class as Jews on a train in Poland, confronting the group about the historical implications. He disrupts James's tour presentation about Lublin destinations

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: David searches through crowded train cars looking for Benji after he left the first class compartment

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND TRAIN CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: David finds Benji sitting alone in a second train car. Benji expresses frustration that everyone wants a 'joyride' and people can't just be happy all the time while dealing with serious history

scenes.heading: EXT. POLAND COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: External shots of the train speeding through various Polish countryside locations

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: David falls asleep on the train with Benji watching him. When David wakes at a station, they prepare to disembark together

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNIK PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins emerge at Krasnik platform and realize they missed their Lublin stop. Benji admits he let David sleep through their destination, leading to David's frustration about Benji's priorities

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji outlines an elaborate plan to avoid paying for return tickets by evading the conductor, using anti-corporate rhetoric to justify their ticket dodging scheme

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins successfully reach first class seats on the return train. Despite David pointing out the irony of ending up in first class again, Benji feels they've earned it

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STATION — DAY
scenes.summary: The train arrives at Lublin Glowny Station and the boys walk along the platform looking for the exit

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji and David emerge to find James and the irritated tour group waiting. Benji immediately blames David for falling asleep

scenes.heading: EXT. STAIRS TO CASTLE - DAY
scenes.summary: James leads the group up castle stairs, describing Lublin as 'The Jewish Oxford' - a diverse, progressive, welcoming city with rich Jewish intellectual and cultural history

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIDGE FROM ZAMKOWA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David questions Eloge about his religious practices while Marcia approaches Benji to discuss his train comments about understanding suffering. She shares concerns about her wealthy son-in-law

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Montage of hidden Jewish historical sites around Lublin accompanied by James's voiceover about memories trapped in amber - former theaters, medical centers, and other unmarked locations

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVEL STREET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The group walks along an uneven gravel path at town's edge. Benji throws his arm around David and pinches his cheek in an affectionate moment

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY – DUSK
scenes.summary: At the Jewish cemetery dating to 1541, James discusses historical significance while Benji becomes visibly annoyed with the constant lecturing. Tension builds as Benji approaches James and Eloge

scenes.heading: EXT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of a lively restaurant in old town Lublin at night

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During dinner with Hava Nagila playing, Benji erupts about antisemitic atmosphere and 'essentializing' Jewish history. His outburst disrupts Marcia's family story, leading to his dramatic exit
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's explosive confrontation reveals his deep anger about trivializing Jewish suffering

scenes.heading: EXT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David emerges from the restaurant alone, hearing Benji's music becoming insistent and grating as it follows him psychologically on his walk back to the hotel

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ENTRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David enters the Lublin hotel while Benji's music continues to play in his head, representing his psychological state

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Alone in his room, David takes anxiety medication, tries unsuccessfully to call Priya, and examines his bare feet while contemplating his situation

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Middle of the night, David pretends to sleep as Benji enters, rifles through belongings, and leaves at 2:40 AM, leaving David confused and concerned

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: David checks the empty hallway for Benji after his mysterious departure

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David hurriedly gets dressed, irritated and worried about Benji's disappearance

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HALLWAY - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David searches the hotel hallway looking for Benji

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN LOBBY - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David asks the front desk about Benji, but they haven't seen him

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David lies awake in bed, both angry and worried about Benji's whereabouts

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: After sleeping sitting up, David is startled by a phone call from Benji saying everyone's waiting downstairs

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ELEVATOR – MORNING
scenes.summary: David rides the elevator down, visibly angry about the previous night

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN LOBBY — MORNING
scenes.summary: David confronts Benji about his disappearance. Benji casually explains he was with Marcia and didn't want to wake David, showing no awareness of David's distress

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: James addresses the group about the challenging day ahead visiting a concentration camp, explaining the broad spectrum of emotional reactions people experience

scenes.heading: EXT. MAJDANEK - DAY
scenes.summary: The bus arrives at Majdanek concentration camp parking lot. The preserved camp appears eerily sparse and unadorned compared to other Holocaust museum sites
scenes.turningPointNote: Arrival at Majdanek marks the emotional climax of their historical journey

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: James explains Majdanek's proximity to Lublin town square - only two miles away - as the group comprehends the scope and unsettling nature of the preserved camp

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: James describes how the Nazis' hasty evacuation left Majdanek uniquely well-preserved as evidence. The cousins are visibly awed by the enormity of the place

scenes.heading: INT. BARRACKS - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside the barracks built for 250 but often housing 500-1000 people. James reads survivor testimony to the group

scenes.heading: INT. UNDRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group stands silently in the bare, windowless undressing room as James identifies the space

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the dank, low-ceiling shower room, James continues reading harrowing personal accounts from survivors

scenes.heading: INT. GAS CHAMBER - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside the gas chamber, James points out blue Zyklon B stains on walls. The group experiences various stages of nausea as Benji unconsciously reaches for David's shoulder
scenes.turningPointNote: The gas chamber represents the peak of horror and brings the cousins together

scenes.heading: INT. CREMATORIUM – DAY
scenes.summary: The group silently observes the perfectly preserved body-sized crematorium ovens. James offers no commentary

scenes.heading: INT. SHOE DISPLAY BARRACK - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji and David stand together before a display of victims' shoes, overwhelmed by the visual representation of mass murder

scenes.heading: EXT. MAUSOLEUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group stands dwarfed beneath the enormous spaceship-like Mausoleum structure

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Returning to the hotel in shell-shocked silence, David sees Benji openly crying while staring out the window, breaking David's heart
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's raw emotional breakdown reveals his deep capacity for feeling and connection to suffering

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The emotionally drained group walks toward the hotel entrance

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: James facilitates group processing in the hotel lobby. The group acknowledges feeling nauseous and overwhelmed. James announces Benji and David's departure from the tour
scenes.turningPointNote: The cousins' departure marks their transition from group experience to private reckoning

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The cousins walk around Lublin together with a noticeable thawing in their relationship, walking above the Czechówka River mural

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTDOOR MARKET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At an outdoor market, Benji tries on a Polish folk hat. Despite David's logical argument against buying it, Benji purchases it anyway

scenes.heading: EXT. HILL OVERLOOKING CITY - DUSK
scenes.summary: The cousins eat greasy shawarma and climb a hill as the sun sets behind them

scenes.heading: EXT. BREWERY STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Walking under illuminated flowers and butterflies on Brewery Street, Benji playfully jumps to swat a butterfly while David laughs

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STREET OFFICE BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji and David arrive at a tall office building on the outskirts of Lublin at night. Benji reveals he has one last joint and they plan to access the building's roof by following a smoker inside.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN BUILDING LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins successfully sneak into the building lobby by following the smoker past an indifferent security guard to the elevator bank.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the elevator to the top floor, Benji and David endure awkward silence with the Polish smoker who makes small talk in Polish about tomorrow's rain.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins emerge onto the quiet rooftop overlooking Lublin after struggling with a rusty industrial door.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF – NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the rooftop, Benji and David share a joint while overlooking the city. David spots the Majdanek concentration camp in the distance, creating a somber moment. When David asks about Benji's future plans in Binghamton, Benji deflects and asks for the joint.
scenes.turningPointNote: The visual confrontation with the concentration camp and David's probing about Benji's future crystallizes their unresolved issues

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins return to their hotel in slow motion, suggesting a contemplative or altered state from their rooftop experience.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Back in their hotel room, the cousins maintain silence. They take turns showering and go to sleep in separate beds, the mood notably bleak between them.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: The next morning, David and Benji pack their belongings without speaking to each other.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY DINING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins eat breakfast in silence at the hotel dining area until David's phone buzzes with their taxi arrival notification.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji load their bags into a colorful taxi outside the hotel lobby.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi travels through the Polish countryside, passing a road marker for Krasnystaw.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins ride silently in the back of the taxi on their way to their grandmother's hometown.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNYSTAW TOWN SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi arrives at Krasnystaw's quiet town square, surrounded by shops and simple three-story apartment buildings. The cousins retrieve their luggage.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE – DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji search the town square for address number 25, with David consulting his phone for directions.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET NEAR CHURCH - DAY
scenes.summary: David leads Benji around a corner past St. Francis Xavier Church, which has a massive wooden cross in its parking lot, toward an alley.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY IN FRONT OF HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins find their grandmother's childhood home at address 25 in an unchanged alley, but feel underwhelmed by its unremarkable appearance. Benji shares a memory of their grandmother slapping him for being late and high to dinner, describing it as the best thing that ever happened to him because it showed how much she cared.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of their grandmother's home and Benji's vulnerable memory about being slapped represents a key emotional breakthrough about love and care

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNYTAW TOWN SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk through the town square with their bags and exit frame, leaving the town to continue without them.

scenes.heading: I/E. TAXI - NEAR KRASNYSTAW - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Benji ride in a taxi heading back to Warsaw Airport.

scenes.heading: I/E. TAXI - WARSAW
scenes.summary: The taxi approaches Warsaw Chopin Airport and takes the exit.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk through Warsaw Airport without speaking but appear comfortable in each other's presence.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: On the airplane home, both David and Benji are awake and lost in their own thoughts.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK GATE – DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins exit their flight gate at JFK Airport with other passengers and walk through the terminal.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK BAGGAGE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: At JFK baggage area, David invites Benji for dinner with his family, but Benji declines. When David offers to share a cab and drop Benji at Penn Station, Benji surprisingly chooses to stay at the airport alone, claiming he likes meeting people there.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's decision to remain at the airport rather than reconnect with family represents his continued isolation and avoidance of stability

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - DAY
scenes.summary: David rides alone in a cab heading home from the airport, looking out the window.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - DAY
scenes.summary: David's cab passes LeFrak City and the massive Machpelah cemetery, representing Jewish history, upward mobility, and death. David takes it all in as part of his heritage.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David walks down his Brooklyn street toward his apartment, stopping to place a memorial stone on his stoop.
scenes.turningPointNote: The placement of the memorial stone represents David's integration of his grief and heritage into his daily life

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: David enters his apartment and calls out to his family. Priya and his son Abe run to embrace him, and he lifts Abe into the air and kisses his wife, surrounded by the warmth of his seemingly nice life.
scenes.turningPointNote: David's return to his loving family contrasts sharply with Benji's isolation, highlighting their different coping mechanisms

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: Back at JFK Airport, in a mirror of the opening shot, the camera slowly zooms through crowds of travelers to reveal Benji sitting alone on the same airport bench. He watches strangers pass by, and the film's title reappears over his face as the camera moves closer.
scenes.turningPointNote: The circular ending with Benji alone at the airport emphasizes his continued state of limbo and emotional paralysis, bringing the story full circle
